title: Johnson & Johnson Q1 2026 Earnings Highlights
text: Johnson & Johnson reported first-quarter 2026 sales of $24.1 billion, up 9.9% year over year on a reported basis, with operational growth of 6.4% and adjusted operational growth of 5.3%. Diluted EPS was $2.14, while adjusted diluted EPS was $2.70. Innovative Medicine sales were $15.4 billion, up 11.2%, and MedTech sales were $8.6 billion, up 7.7%. Management highlighted progress from recent approvals, including ICOTYDE for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, TECVAYLI plus DARZALEX FASPRO for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, VARIPULSE Pro in Europe, and TECNIS PureSee in the United States. Johnson & Johnson also raised its 2026 outlook, with estimated reported sales of about $100.8 billion at the midpoint and adjusted EPS of about $11.55 at the midpoint. The results show a strong start to 2026, with growth driven by both medicine and medical technology innovation.
